name: CVF Governance Anti-Rationalization Guard
owner: CVF governance review and closure surface
purpose: Surface shortcut-claim patterns that require source verification, tests, review, or safe stop before a governed agent may close work.
useWhen: A worker, reviewer, or closer sees language that minimizes source verification, testing, evidence, scope, or review requirements.
doNotUseWhen: The claim is already backed by source verification, current gate receipts, and an explicit closure boundary.
executionConstraints: metadata-only candidate; does not implement a machine checker or auto-blocking runtime behavior
acceptanceEvidence: future promotion requires repeated defect evidence, package instance proof, and reviewer acceptance
authorityCeiling: metadata-only review signal; cannot block or pass work without existing CVF gates or reviewer decision
permissions: read metadata and cited governed source artifacts only
rollback: delete this registry entry and regenerate the skill index if reviewer rejects AGSK-T7
safeStop: if rationalization signals are present and evidence is absent, stop and require source verification or reviewer decision
capabilityBoundary: no checker wiring, no runtime block, no package activation, no authority expansion beyond reviewer decision
resolverBehavior: metadata-only; resolver returns identity and selector fields; does not open any SKILL.md or instruction body
loaderBoundary: loading this metadata never grants authority to skip evidence, override gates, or claim closure
adapterContract: N/A with reason: external CLI/MCP adapter not authored in AGSK-T7
adapterEvidence: N/A with reason: no adapter implemented in AGSK-T7
externalMutationBoundary: no external mutation permitted until a separate ASSF adapter work order is accepted
shellAssumptions: no shell execution required for metadata-only candidate
